description: Update the shared relational API metadata formatter so `_etag` derivation recursively removes every server-generated field named `id`, `link`, `_etag`, or `_lastModifiedDate` before canonical JSON serialization. Acceptance: relational resources and descriptors use the same canonicalization path; `link` subtrees at root, reference, collection, nested collection, and `_ext` locations never affect the hash; real resource-state value changes still produce a different hash; existing metadata injection and readable-profile `RefreshEtag` behavior continue to work.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/RelationalApiMetadataFormatterTests.cs` to confirm coverage for nested `link`, root metadata, descriptor bodies, and a real reference identity value change.

description: Add a typed `If-Match`/write-precondition contract and use it instead of direct backend header reads. Acceptance: Core constructs the contract once from request headers using exact opaque-string semantics; absent means the header key is not present after frontend filtering; any non-dropped blank/whitespace value is treated as present and mismatched; no quote normalization, entity-tag list parsing, splitting, trimming, or malformed-header rejection is performed; relational and descriptor backend code consume only the typed contract, not `Headers["If-Match"]`.
steps-to-verify: Review the added Core and backend precondition tests to confirm coverage for absent, exact, quoted, whitespace, and collapsed multi-value `If-Match` inputs.

Run: `rg "Headers\\[[^]]*\\\"If-Match\\\"|TryGetValue\\(\\\"If-Match\\\"|ContainsKey\\(\\\"If-Match\\\"|Contains\\(\\\"If-Match\\\"" src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core -g '*.cs'` and verify only the Core precondition factory reads the raw header in production code.

description: Add `UpsertResult.UpsertFailureETagMisMatch` and wire all HTTP mappings to the existing 412 optimistic-lock response shape. Acceptance: POST upsert-as-update and POST create-new-with-If-Match can return the new result; `UpsertHandler` maps it to the same `urn:ed-fi:api:optimistic-lock-failed` body used by `UpdateFailureETagMisMatch` and `DeleteFailureETagMisMatch`; retry policy does not treat ETag mismatch as retryable; existing update/delete 412 behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/Handler/UpsertHandlerTests.cs` and `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/Handler/DeadlockRetryPolicyTests.cs` to confirm `UpsertFailureETagMisMatch` maps to the standard 412 response body and is not treated as retryable.

description: Plumb the readable comparison surface needed for `If-Match` through Core/backend relational write contracts. Acceptance: profiled PUT and profiled POST-as-update compare against the readable-profile projected representation the client would have received from a profiled GET and return that same profile-surface ETag on successful profiled writes; no-profile writes compare against the full resource-state representation; DELETE ignores profile media-type headers for precondition purposes and uses the full-resource ETag; writable descriptor profiles either fail before precondition logic if unsupported or explicitly carry the same readable surface if they are supported.
steps-to-verify: Review the added Core request-construction tests to confirm the typed precondition carries readable projection context independently from the writable profile body.

Review the added backend profile-write tests to confirm profiled committed write responses return the projected ETag, DELETE uses the full-resource ETag, and unsupported descriptor profile writes fail before precondition comparison unless explicit support is added.

description: Implement a relational current-representation precondition helper for existing relational-table resources. Acceptance: inside the write transaction, the helper re-resolves or confirms the target, locks the target `dms.Document` row (`FOR UPDATE` for PostgreSQL, `UPDLOCK, HOLDLOCK, ROWLOCK` for SQL Server), hydrates/materializes the current representation, applies the readable profile projection when supplied, computes the link-excluding `_etag`, and compares it exactly to the typed precondition value while holding the row lock through subsequent DML or delete.
steps-to-verify: Review the added backend unit tests to confirm PostgreSQL and SQL Server lock SQL, exact comparison success/failure, and profile-projected comparison are all covered.

description: Enforce optional `If-Match` for relational-table PUT and POST upsert flows in the authoritative in-transaction path. Acceptance: absent `If-Match` preserves existing behavior; exact match allows changed writes and guarded no-op success; mismatch returns `UpdateFailureETagMisMatch` or `UpsertFailureETagMisMatch` before reference resolution, profile creatability validation, merge validation, or DML after target existence and authorization are satisfied; authorization failures still win over `412`; POST resolving to create-new with present `If-Match` returns `UpsertFailureETagMisMatch` once target identity resolution proves the request is an insert, before deeper backend validation, while earlier malformed-request/profile-usage failures still win; PUT missing target remains 404.
steps-to-verify: Review the added executor/repository unit tests to confirm absent-header behavior, exact match, mismatch short-circuit after authorization but before deeper backend validation, authorization-failure precedence, PUT missing-target 404, POST-as-update mismatch, and POST create-new-with-If-Match are all covered.

description: Enforce optional `If-Match` for relational-table DELETE by id. Acceptance: missing target still returns 404; authorization failures still win over `412`; absent `If-Match` preserves existing delete behavior; present exact match locks and compares the current full-resource representation before deleting; present mismatch returns `DeleteFailureETagMisMatch` without issuing the delete; the lock is held through delete/commit; existing FK conflict mapping to 409 remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review the added delete-path unit tests to confirm PostgreSQL and SQL Server lock SQL, exact success, mismatch 412 result, 404 precedence, authorization precedence, and FK conflict preservation are all covered.

description: Change stale guarded no-op handling to honor present `If-Match`. Acceptance: when the shared guarded no-op freshness recheck reports stale and the precondition is present, PUT returns `UpdateFailureETagMisMatch` and POST-as-update returns `UpsertFailureETagMisMatch` immediately with no stale retry; when the precondition is absent, the existing single stale-no-op retry remains unchanged; behavior applies to both no-profile and profiled guarded no-op paths.
steps-to-verify: Review the added stale guarded-no-op unit tests to confirm `DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor` and repository retry behavior differ correctly when a precondition is present versus absent.

description: Refactor descriptor POST and PUT onto a shared descriptor current-state/precondition helper so they enforce the same typed optional `If-Match` semantics as relational-table resources. Acceptance: `DescriptorWriteRequest` carries the typed precondition plus any readable comparison surface needed for supported descriptor write modes; descriptor POST resolving to create-new with present `If-Match` returns `UpsertFailureETagMisMatch`; descriptor POST-as-update and PUT use the shared helper to lock the owning `dms.Document` row, materialize the current descriptor resource-state representation, compute the exact current ETag, and compare it exactly; existing-target mismatch short-circuits after existence and authorization are satisfied but before deeper validation or DML; absent `If-Match` preserves existing behavior; unsupported descriptor profile writes fail before precondition logic unless explicit support is added with the readable comparison surface; if the shared current-state comparison makes descriptor POST-as-update unchanged detection trivial while touching this code, reuse it to short-circuit that overlap without extra stamp/journal work, but leave broader descriptor stamp/journal hardening in `DMS-1008`; identity/URI immutability behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/DescriptorWriteHandlerDeleteTests.cs`, `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/DescriptorWriteHandlerNoOpTests.cs`, and any added descriptor write tests to confirm absent precondition, exact match, mismatch short-circuit after authorization but before deeper validation, POST create-new-with-If-Match, POST-as-update exact/mismatch and unchanged-existing overlap short-circuit, immutable URI changes, and unsupported profiled descriptor writes are all covered.

description: Enforce optional `If-Match` for descriptor DELETE. Acceptance: descriptor delete carries a typed precondition rather than raw headers and reuses the shared descriptor current-state/precondition helper instead of duplicating lock/compare logic; missing descriptor target still returns 404; authorization failures still win over `412`; DELETE compares against the full descriptor-resource ETag and ignores profile media-type headers for precondition purposes; absent `If-Match` preserves existing descriptor delete behavior; exact match locks the owning `dms.Document` row, materializes the current descriptor representation, and allows delete; mismatch returns `DeleteFailureETagMisMatch` without issuing delete; descriptor FK conflict mapping remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/DescriptorWriteHandlerDeleteTests.cs` and any added delete-path tests to confirm absent precondition, exact match, mismatch, missing-target precedence, authorization precedence, DELETE full-resource ETag behavior, and FK conflict preservation are all covered.

description: Add coverage proving resource links do not affect `If-Match`. Acceptance: a client ETag obtained with `DataManagement:ResourceLinks:Enabled=true` succeeds when the write precondition check runs against a link-stripped representation; a client ETag obtained with links disabled succeeds when the backend has a link-bearing intermediate document; flag-on and flag-off responses for the same resource-state surface produce identical `_etag` values; any failures are fixed in the shared canonicalization or response strip path, not by special-casing `If-Match`.
steps-to-verify: Review the added unit and integration/E2E tests to confirm link-bearing versus link-stripped canonicalization and both `DataManagement:ResourceLinks:Enabled` flag states are covered without special-casing `If-Match`.

description: Add cross-dialect relational integration tests proving dependency identity changes make stale `If-Match` fail. Acceptance: using the small referential-identity fixture, create Student and dependent ResourceA, capture ResourceA `_etag`, update Student.studentUniqueId with `allowIdentityUpdates=true`, verify ResourceA's served `_etag` changed due to propagated reference identity values, then assert a stale `If-Match` on ResourceA PUT returns 412 for both PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
steps-to-verify: Review the added PostgreSQL and SQL Server integration tests to confirm they use Student -> ResourceA from the small referential-identity fixtures and assert both ETag change and stale `If-Match` 412 behavior.

description: Move or retag the existing resource `If-Match` E2E scenarios so they execute against the relational backend without reducing scenario coverage. Acceptance: the scenarios in `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Resources/etag.feature` still cover retrieving ETag, PUT exact match, request-body `_etag` ignored, PUT mismatch 412, DELETE mismatch 412, and DELETE exact match; the relational backend is enabled for this lane; setup/teardown uses the DMS E2E local stack instructions.
steps-to-verify: Review `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Resources/etag.feature` and its generated test metadata to confirm the feature runs in the relational lane (`@relational-backend` tagging or equivalent lane configuration) and still covers the same six scenarios.
